title: Watch: Fans react to tense World Cup game
description: England Captain Harry Kane rescued England from a major upset, with two late goals, after DR Congo took an early lead.

title: Quiz of the Week: Have you been following this week's news?
description: It's been a busy week of big stories and headlines. Have you been keeping up with the news over the last seven days? Test your knowledge of the latest top stories with our Quiz of the Week.

title: Lone star ticks are covering much of the U.S. Here's what you need to know
description: It's a tick that hunts you down and transmits a potentially dangerous allergy to red meat. The New Yorker writer Burkhard Bilger discusses the lone star tick and the risks of alpha-gal syndrome.

title: High Tech Jacket Prototype Pulls Drinking Water From Thin Air – Up to 1.5 Pints Per Day
description: A new high tech jacket developed by engineers at the University of Texas can pull drinking water from thin air. With the advance in fabric technology, the jacket can collect up to one-and-a-half pints

title: Antarctica's first dinosaur fossil belonged to a group of the largest land animals ever
description: Researchers have identified the first-ever dinosaur fossil discovered on Antarctica, revealing it belonged to a titanosaur.

title: A whopping 14 million species of insects — or more — may roam Earth
description: New calculations suggest that the insect species inhabiting our planet may be double or triple previous estimates.

title: Why does metal stick together in space?
description: If you push two metal plates together on Earth, nothing happens. In space, they can fuse into one. Here's why.

title: Scientists Say: Fluoride
description: This electrically charged form of fluorine boasts powerful tooth-strengthening abilities.

title: Tennis superstar makes a big entrance at Wimbledon
